sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the character is now at the bottom right, near the yellow L-shape. The dark gray L-shape is at the bottom left. The yellow L-shape is at rows 45-53, cols 51-59.
The character is at the bottom right, near the yellow timer bar. The character is at rows ~57-63, cols ~54-62.
The yellow L-shape is at rows 45-53, cols 51-59. The character is below the yellow L-shape.
I need to move the character to the yellow L-shape's position. The character is at rows 57-63, and the yellow L-shape is at rows 45-53. So I need to move UP 10 steps.
But wait, the character is also to the right of the yellow L-shape. The character is at cols 54-62, and the yellow L-shape is at cols 51-59. So the character is slightly to the right.
Actually, looking at the image, the character is at the bottom right, below the yellow L-shape. The character's body is black with white eyes.
The yellow L-shape is at rows 45-53, cols 51-59. The character is at rows 57-63, cols 54-62.
I need to move UP 10 steps to reach the yellow L-shape's row. The character is already at the correct column (cols 54-62 vs cols 51-59).
Let me try UP 10 to reach the yellow L-shape's position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Character at bottom right, need to move UP to yellow L's row
# Yellow L at rows 45-53, character at rows ~57-63
# UP 10 to reach row 47 (middle of yellow L's row range)
action(['UP'] * 10)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
